name: Clam Chowder-- Maine Style
recipeInstructions:
1. Dice the onions and salt pork.
2. Sauté slowly in an iron frying pan until the meat is browned.
3. Empty the contents of the frying pan into a chowder pot.
4. Add 1 quart (or more) of water to the pot.
5. Bring the mixture to a boil, then add the diced potatoes and return to a boil.
6. Pass the clams through a meat grinder or food mill and stir them into the pot.
7. Simmer until the potatoes are tender.
8. Season with salt and pepper.
9. Stir in 12 fluid ounces of evaporated milk.
10. Turn off the heat and let the chowder set for a few minutes.
11. Reheat just before serving.
12. Serve with crackers. (Note: Canned chopped clams can be used if fresh ones are unavailable.)
